entry.title: Re: Welcome to Career Corner
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Afi Elom Konutse. Thank you for having me here! im Excited to be part of this community. I have a background in Business administration, marketing and event coordination. I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, i would like to be an SAP consultant with a specialization in sales and distribution.

Can you give me some advice on what certifications, hands on practices and experiences i need to be marketable? Thank you

Best regards,

Afi_Elom

@craigcmehil @RichBlumberg @Jason_Cao

entry.title: Re: Proration and Number Formats in Variable Pay
entry.content:
Hi @dorota_g 

I won't have time to test on this any time soon due to other commitments. 

Please try #,##0.000000 then run bonus calculation again and relaunch the worksheet (mandatory steps after you change number formats to retest).

If it works as I would expect and you can see a lot of decimals, then start reverting back until you have the number of decimals you want.

I wish Variable Pay was more of a predictable module with clear answers to all questions but even when I am confronted with these issues I apply the steps above.

Good luck and please let me know.

Xavier

entry.title: How to get the API Key for S4 HANA Cloud trial (Not for the public sandbox).
entry.content:
Hey all, I've been struggling to find how to get the API key for my trial of S4 HANA Cloud.

I've only found how to retrieve the API Key that let's you hit this url: https://sandbox.api.sap.com
But where do I get the key for my S4 HANA Cloud trial instance that has a url of this style: https://myXXXXX.s4hana.ondemand.com/ ?

I'd appreciate a lot some help, also if you can mention how I'd get the API Key for a production environment as well, would be helpful in advance. Cheers.

entry.title: Re: How to Edit Multiple Components Simultaneously in SAP Build Apps’ Logic Canvas?
entry.content:
Hello @Dan_Wroblewski, thank you so much for the quick response!

1) At first, I controlled the visibility of components through several page variables. However, after an update, whenever a variable starts as “false,” the component becomes invisible in the user interface, which greatly complicates the development process. In my case, each page has a variety of components that sometimes need to be displayed and sometimes hidden. When they start off invisible, not being able to see them directly in the UI slows down the work. That’s why I chose to use the “Hide component” and “Show component” features, which don’t have this issue. The new challenge is that I now use dozens of them per page, and if it were possible to edit their properties to control multiple components at once, the logical flow would be much simpler and more organized.

2) Exactly, I’m referring to the “Hide component” and “Show component” features. It would be extremely useful to add more than one input, allowing the visibility of multiple components to be managed simultaneously, making the process more efficient.

entry.title: Re: While trying to select 'Entity Set' in 'Gateway Clien...
entry.content: have you registered your service  in /n/iwfnd/maint_service?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Welcome!

entry.title: failover and plan cache
entry.content: If we are doing a failover of HANA from node a to node b and if node b is the new primary server in the HSR scenario, will this result in new PLAN_ID's getting generated and complete sql plan cache rebuilt. Just asking for guidance

entry.title: Re: Finding B1 UI Field Labels for Database Fields Withou...
entry.content:
Dear @jun_dolor,

If you have installed SDK, then look for SDK help file, it describes the tables with field name, field description and its functionality. This will show only that are exposed in Business One.

Thanks.

entry.title: Introducing the SAP Cloud SDK for AI (Java) 🎉
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce the initial release of the SAP Cloud SDK for AI for Java! Only a few weeks ago we already announced the release of the JavaScript/TypeScript variant. Similarly, this SDK for Java enables convenient integration of generative AI capabilities within your SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) applications and allows you to utilize the Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core.

For SAP BTP developers, the AI SDK is crafted to simplify AI integration and enhance application impact. With features that optimize deployment, improve content safety, and facilitate model orchestration, the SDK lets you bring advanced AI functionality to your applications swiftly and with minimal setup. Whether you need adaptable workflows, secure data handling, or smooth generative model integration, the SDK equips you with robust tools to embed AI-powered features in your SAP BTP solutions.

This post introduces the main modules and their features.

 

AI Core - Setup and Usage

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk</groupId>
  <artifactId>core</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>

Automate tasks such as creating AI Core artifacts, configurations, and deployments, executing batch inference jobs, as well as managing Docker registries and object storage for training data. The core module provides tools for workflow and scenario management within SAP AI Core.

  • Artifact management: register and organize datasets and model artifacts.
  • Configuration management: set up configurations for various models and use cases.
  • Deployment management: deploy AI models and manage their lifecycle within SAP AI Core.

Example SDK code: Create a deployment in SAP AI Core.

var api = new DeploymentApi();
var resourceGroupId = "default";
var request = AiDeploymentCreationRequest.create().configurationId("12345-123-123-123-123456abcdefg");

AiDeploymentCreationResponse deployment = api.create(resourceGroupId, request);
String id = deployment.getId();
AiExecutionStatus status = deployment.getStatus();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for SAP AI Core in the public repository guide.
 

AI Core - Orchestration

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk</groupId>
  <artifactId>orchestration</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
This orchestration module lets you use the Generative AI Hub Orchestration Service with templating, content filtering, and data masking from within your applications. The Orchestration Service streamlines AI interactions while ensuring adherence to content safety guidelines.
  • Templating: Build dynamic prompts with placeholders to tailor AI interactions to user inputs.
  • Content Filtering: Apply filters to maintain compliance with content safety guidelines.
  • Data Masking: Anonymize and pseudonymize sensitive data.
  • Grounding: Add external data sources for contextually relevant information (planned for Q1 2025).

Example SDK code: Write a simple chat completion.

var client = new OrchestrationClient();
var config = new OrchestrationModuleConfig().withLlmConfig(OrchestrationAiModel.GPT_4O);
var prompt = new OrchestrationPrompt("Hello world! Why is this phrase so famous?");
var result = client.chatCompletion(prompt, config);

String messageResult = result.getContent();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for Orchestration Service in the public repository guide.
 

AI Core - Foundation Models

<dependency>
  <groupId>com.sap.ai.sdk.foundationmodels</groupId>
  <artifactId>openai</artifactId>
  <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
The openai module, along with other modules in the com.sap.ai.sdk.foundationmodels group, enables streamlined access to specific generative AI models available through the Generative AI Hub. The module provides a simplified interface focused on direct model interactions, ideal for developers who require direct access to foundation models for inference and embedding requests without additional orchestration features.

Currently only openai is supported. Please open a feature request, if you need direct LLM access for other foundation models.

Example SDK code: Write a simple chat completion.

var result = 
  OpenAiClient.forModel(GPT_35_TURBO)
    .withSystemPrompt("You are a helpful AI")
    .chatCompletion("Hello World! Why is this phrase so famous?");

String resultMessage = result.getContent();
You can learn more about the SDK's capabilities for foundation models and OpenAI specific features in the public repository guide.
 

Getting Started

You will need Java 17 or higher. Spring Boot or SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) as a framework is recommended, but not required. To explore these packages further, check out our sample project, which shows the usage of the various SDK packages.

 

Support and Feedback

We’d love your feedback on this first release! For support or to share your ideas, feel free to open an issue on GitHub.

 

Latest News

  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI for JavaScript released in October 2024.
  • Visit sap.com/ai and explore our portfolio
  • Explore the available AI capabilities on SAP Discovery Center.
  • Discover the latest announcements in the SAP TechEd Press Release and the SAP TechEd News Guide.
  • Review the SAP Road Map Explorer for a detailed view of upcoming product innovations.
  • Join the SAP Community page to connect with experts and share knowledge.

entry.title: NWBC for HTML Will Be Discontinued
entry.content:
The NWBC for HTML (NetWeaver Business Client for HTML) has been on a long journey and it is time to bring this to an end. Starting with S/4HANA 2025 it will be discontinued. But you will not be kept alone—there are two alternatives you can choose from:

  • Fiori Launchpad (FLP): Now this is the right moment to switch to FLP.
  • SAP Business Client (SAP BC) aka NWBC for Desktop: This is a desktop application for Microsoft Windows in which you can use the old roles and where you have some advantages over the NWBC for HTML: 
    • Multiple tabs to work with various web-based applications and Dynpro transactions in parallel 
    • Favorites 
    • Context-sensitive sidepanel 
    • Favourites 
    • Overview of most viewed applications and recently used applications 
    • Accessing Dynpro transactions via SAPGUI for Windows 
    • Harmonized UI, including a themed shell 

On this journey the names of the products changed a little bit. Therefore, it is understandable that there might be some confusion. Let's try to clarify this:

  • In the beginning there was the NWBC for HTML and the NWBC for Desktop. Talking about the NWBC was sometimes ambiguous because of the different products with nearly the same name.
  • With version 6.0 the NWBC for Desktop was renamed to SAP Business Client for Desktop (SAP BC) while the NWBC for HTML stayed with its name.
  • So now there is the SAP Business Client for Desktop (in the meantime with version 8.00) and the NWBC for HTML.
  • If you hear about the discontinuation of the NWBC this is only about the NWBC for HTML, not about the SAP BC! The SAP BC will continue.

If you have many custom roles defined for your NWBC you can think about switching to the SAP BC because all the old roles can be used there. You don't need to change them—just check the additional features which are perhaps helpful for you and your users, e.g. own Menu entries, Search Providers or Side Panels which can provide additional information to existing applications.

A disadvantage could be that the SAP BC is a Windows application which has to be installed on every relevant machine. Find information about the SAP BC in the SAP Help Portal.

The other option is switching to the SAP Fiori Launchpad. This is the most current shell of SAP. It runs in the browser, so you don't have to install something on every computer in your environment. If you use custom roles, you will probably need to make yourself familiar with the new role concept and create new roles for your environment. In the SAP Help Portal you will find more information about this.

Be aware that you also can run the FLP within the SAP BC. More about this you can also find in the SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: Success factors for custom AI use cases: from the idea to the productive phase
entry.content:
The question of whether or not the use of AI in a company can be a value-added booster has long been answered - in fact, it is almost negligent not to consider the use of AI in one's company. Nevertheless, our experience shows that only a portion of the AI use case ideas considered for implementation ultimately makes it to the productive phase. At SAP, we understand that the introduction of AI use cases is a challenging journey into often unfamiliar territory. This makes it even more important to have an approach that provides clear orientation.

A lifecycle stage model for categorizing AI use cases is a critical tool for navigating the challenges of AI adoption and ensuring successful implementation. It offers several advantages: Many initiatives fail due to technical difficulties, unclear goals, or a lack of user acceptance. By applying a staged approach, resources can be allocated more effectively, risks identified early, and focus directed toward promising projects. This ensures that AI use cases are efficiently developed, from the initial idea to impactful deployment. Additionally, it serves as an excellent methodology for monitoring whether there are sufficient ideas and use cases in the early stages to meet company KPIs, such as achieving a specific number of go-live use cases each year.

These insights are based on experience within a successful AI program at a client and are specifically true for custom AI use cases. It’s important to note that “Embedded” or “Joule AI” use cases can also be classified within the same stage structure, but it might be possible to start with them directly at a later stage or skip certain stages in between.

Introducing the five stages lifecycle model - from exploration to roll-out
As illustrated in Fig. 1, the lifecycle stage model, consisting of five stages, can be divided into two phases. The early stages require relatively fewer resources and less technological investment and focus on evaluating whether a use case is viable for productive deployment. If these early stages are successful and a use case is considered beneficial, the mature stages focused on productive use can follow.

Figure 1: Five stages lifecycle model

Early stages: From the idea to the prototype
In the early stages of an AI use case, the focus is on defining the user group, clarifying the intended benefits, and identifying potential risks. In addition, consideration is given to its integration into end-to-end processes. This structured approach ensures a lean start with minimal use of resources and reduces the risk of unnecessary expenditure. The specific characteristics of each stage are described below:

1. Exploration
In the exploration phase, use cases are evaluated based on their potential benefits, effort, and risk. The primary task in this phase is to identify relevant and feasible AI use cases. However, this assessment is initially only a rough estimate, without a clear concept, an examination of the underlying data quality, and an analysis of the end-to-end process.

2. Low Fidelity Prototype
The focus here is on the concept. Workshops, such as design thinking, are used to define the target group, benefits and process integration. The aim is to assess the fundamental feasibility, viability and desirability. An essential component is to obtain a clear picture of the underlying data quality in order to identify potential obstacles at an early stage. Additionally, it provides an excellent opportunity to rethink and transform business processes through the use of AI.

3. Functional Prototype
Based on the findings, a functional prototype has been developed to evaluate its technical feasibility. Furthermore, the AI model must be trained and improved, which is usually a key challenge. During the technical implementation, however, the focus is less on perfecting of the prototype but more on validating the concept of the LoFi prototype. This step ensures efficient resource management and prepares the transition to a possible, more resource-intensive productive use.

From validation to scaling: the path to productive use
If the early stages lead to the conclusion of a positive use case, the mature stages follow, which are characterized by productive use. This refers to use in day-to-day business. How these two phases of productive use differ is described as follows:  

4. Pilot
The aim here is to achieve productive use, which requires greater effort, perfection and technical robustness. Additionally, process integration and change management represent the key challenges in this phase. However, meaningful further development in these phases only takes place if the benefits of the use case have been sufficiently demonstrated in the previous phases (e.g. through the functional prototype).

5. Roll-out
The roll-out throughout the company takes place as soon as the use case has been successfully tested in the previous phases and confirmed to be useful for productive use, with a continued focus on change management and ensuring a consumer-ready AI. In order to avoid disruptions in day-to-day business, the use case should have been prepared in a stable and reliable manner.

This maturity level structure described above provides many advantages for the stability of your AI use case. You will have identified potential obstacles at an early stage. You will have ensured efficient resource management and you will be prepared to transition to a possible, more resource-intensive productive use. The functional prototype sufficiently demonstrates its use, and when productive, you can be confident that disruptions will be avoided in day-to-day business.

Shaping Your entire AI Program with the Lifecycle Stage Structure
In addition to the already mentioned advantages of a maturity level structure, this structure can provide another important strategic benefit for companies with ambitious AI goals that are developing several AI use cases in parallel.

Being able to observe the lifecycle during the stages enables systematic and successful further development of the entire portfolio.[1] For example, with an overview of the number of use cases in each phase, important strategic AI KPIs - such as the introduction of a certain number of productive use cases each year - can be realistically evaluated. This provides a fantastic overview of whether there are enough use cases in the early-stage pipeline to achieve the company's goal of bringing a certain number of use cases into productive use, considering that only a portion of use cases progress from idea to productive use. If necessary, targeted actions such as exploration workshops to come up with new use cases can be initiated to unlock new potential.

To significantly increase the number of AI use cases that go live, organizations must also prioritize certain critical factors. Success at this level requires more than just structure and ambition - it requires a focus on the fundamental phases of the AI use case lifecycle. In particular, the first three phases - exploration, low-fidelity prototype (LoFi) and functional prototype - are critical to future success. These early phases lay the foundation for overcoming challenges and ensure that the use cases can be deployed smoothly.

The following key success factors, derived from practical examples of custom AI use cases, highlight what organizations must focus on to turn their ambitions into reality:

  • Data understanding and data quality: A solid database and data knowledge are essential for predicting potential difficulties when designing implementation options.
  • Early error culture and agile project management: Trial-and-error approaches help to identify weaknesses at an early stage. Weekly planning and interim goals create flexibility and a clear overview. Agile development should be used especially from the creation of a functional prototype onward in order to enable early testing and react more quickly to new or changing findings.
  • Design thinking: The use of the design thinking methodology when creating a low-fidelity prototype enables user-centered development that identifies weaknesses at an early stage. It also promotes interdisciplinary collaboration and thus ensures creative solutions that simultaneously question the current process and streamline it where necessary.
  • Business involvement: Involving the business at an early stage provides realistic insights and improves subsequent acceptance. Especially when using design thinking, it helps enormously in the creation of personas or the as-is analysis to carry out a well-founded assessment.
  • Standardized assets and test data: Templates for carrying out the methodology and early prepared test data are real accelerators, as they enable the prototype phase to be completed more quickly through smart preparation and follow-up work from the workshops and prevent delays during testing
  • Clear leadership and stakeholder communication: Consistent communication and coordinated project management promote transparency and acceptance, especially in cooperation projects with partners who provide for instance, AI solutions for the respective use case.

Your journey to success
A lifecycle stage structure for AI use cases ensures transparency, efficiency and optimized resource management. It not only helps to bring projects successfully to the productive phase, but also to identify risks and challenges at portfolio level at an early stage. This paves the way for the sustainable and scalable use of AI.

The AI race is already well underway. Don’t miss the chance to approach this transformative topic in a structured manner and unlock the opportunities AI has to offer. Leverage the framework and recommendations outlined in this article to enhance your current or future AI use cases. If you’re finding it challenging to get started or feel stuck along the way, SAP Advisory Services is here to support you. Whether it’s through our use case exploration workshops or our service to develop a tailored AI strategy, we’ll gladly guide you on this exciting journey.

 

[1] AI Strategy Approach | Dr. Norbert Enge | SAP Business Transformation & Architecture

entry.title: OData API Versions: A Comparative Analysis of v2 and v4
entry.content:
1. Data Types and Modeling:

  • OData v2: Supports data types such as DateTime, Time, and Float.

OData v4: Introduces additional data types like Date, TimeOfDay, Single, and Enum. It also enhances the entity model with features like inheritance and containment, offering a more robust data modeling framework.

2. Query Capabilities:

  • OData v2: Allows filtering and selection operations on root entities.

OData v4: Expands these capabilities to include filtering, sorting, and selection on expanded entities. It also supports full-text search and user-defined functions, providing more flexible and powerful querying options.

3. Operation Methods:

  • OData v2: Utilizes the HTTP POST method with the X-HTTP-METHOD: MERGE header for data updates.

OData v4: Employs the HTTP PATCH method for updates, aligning with standard HTTP semantics and simplifying partial updates.

4. Performance and Scalability:

OData v4: Offers delta query support, enabling clients to retrieve only changes since the last query. This reduces data transfer and enhances performance, especially in scenarios with large datasets.

5. Support and Future Enhancements:

SAP is progressively focusing on OData v4 as the preferred standard. Future developments and support are expected to concentrate on v4, making it a more future-proof choice.

6. Advantages of the $expand Query Option in OData v4: The $expand system query option in OData v4 allows clients to include related entities inline within the response, facilitating the retrieval of related data in a single request. This reduces the need for multiple round-trips to the server, thereby improving efficiency.

Example: To retrieve a list of categories along with their associated products:

 

 

 

 

GET /odata/Categories?$expand=Products

 

 

This query fetches all categories and their related products in one response.

 

OData v4 enhances the $expand functionality by allowing nested expansions and the application of query options within the expand clause. For instance, to retrieve categories with only the top 5 most expensive products:

 
GET /odata/Categories?$expand=Products($orderby=Price desc;$top=5)

This query returns each category along with its top 5 most expensive products, sorted in descending order by price.

7. Error Responses: Error responses in OData v2 and v4 are formatted in JSON, but v4 provides a more structured and detailed format, aiding in precise error handling and debugging.

OData v2 Error Response Example:{ "error": { "code": "BadRequest", "message": { "lang": "en-US", "value": "Invalid request." } } }OData v4 Error Response Example:

{
  "error": {
    "code": "BadRequest",
    "message": "Invalid request.",
    "target": "query",
    "details": [
      {
        "code": "InvalidFilter",
        "target": "$filter",
        "message": "The filter expression is invalid."
      }
    ],
    "innererror": {
      "trace": [],
      "context": {}
    }
  }
}

The OData v4 error response includes additional fields such as target, details, and innererror, providing more context and facilitating easier troubleshooting.

Conclusion and Recommendation: If your project requires advanced data types, flexible query capabilities, and improved performance, adopting OData v4 APIs is advisable. However, if your existing systems are compatible with OData v2 and meet your current needs, continuing with v2 is acceptable. Considering future support and enhancements, opting for OData v4 for new projects and developments is a more sustainable approach.

For more detailed information, refer to SAP's OData v4 API Reference Guide and OData v2 API Reference Guide.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite – Advanced Scheduler Configuration
entry.content: Also looking for a way to schedule a job every 2 weeks. There is a "Every 14 days starting on" option with then a sub-option to choose the day of the month to start. This is not every 2 weeks. Think about supporting Bi-Weekly pay cycles.

entry.title: The Importance of Sharing Enhancement Ideas with SAP for ME
entry.content:
SAP is committed to continuous improvement, and customer feedback is a vital and valued part of this process. By sharing your ideas, you contribute to the evolution of SAP for ME, making it more powerful and user-friendly.

How to share your Enhancement ideas? Well, that’s easy. All you need to do is to follow these steps:

  • Get started by opening the SAP Customer Influence Open Influence Opportunities - Customer Influence
  • Log on with the S-user and accept the terms of use.
  • Navigate to the SAP for Me Continuous Influence Session (It is recommended to bookmark this link on favorite bar).
  • Search if the idea has already been submitted by another customer and if not, then log the idea.
  • Vote for Ideas that are supported and planned for implementation within the organization. Notifications will be sent about updates on voted ideas.
  • Submit Idea: If an idea that matches the requirement cannot be found, the 'Submit Improvement' button should be used to create a new idea and the relevant template should be filled out.
  • Wait for Review: Once an idea is submitted, it becomes visible to other customers and can be voted on. Ideas that reach the voting threshold (minimum number of votes) will be reviewed.
  • Voting threshold: Each product session may have different thresholds. This information can be found on the session landing page of each product.
  • Ideas that have little to no votes from the community are automatically closed to help ease of navigation & visibility for the community.
  • My Requests / Votes / Comments: All submitted ideas, votes, and comments can be found here.

Are you ready? Well, then we are waiting for you!

SAP for Me is made for you!

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Afi Elom Konutse I’m excited to be a part of SAP Learning Community! I have a background in Business administration, marketing and event coordination. I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group.

My question is would you suggest the Explore Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4 HANA as a follow up course and certification?

I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge and grow with the team.

Thank you for your time and support.

Best Regards,

Afi_Elom

@David_Chaviano @Doris_Lindauer

entry.title: Hi , I have added characteristic values in CT04 in DEV , but when transported to qas i dont see them
entry.content: Hi , I have added characteristic values in CT04 in DEV , but when transported to quality, i dont see them. So i understand that i need to add it manually. For this CT04 is non-editable. What is the other option add one more PGrp to my characteristic values.

entry.title: SAP DM 2200 - ESEF - validation message
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We are using SAP DM stack 2200. We wanted to test new functionalities so we imported our last year's iXBRL package (2023) with the last year's taxonomy in it: base ("base_2023.zip" with entry points 2022-03-24) and extended. We created new package on stack 2200 and new ESEF errors appeared (we didnt have them last year). Is it because it is an old taxonomy and the new taxonomy will solve this problems? 

1. [ESEF.2.6.3.disallowedReportPackageFileExtension] A report package MUST conform to the .ZIP File Format Specification and MUST have a .zip extension.
2. [ESEF.3.1.3.missingOrInvalidTaxonomyPackage] RTS Annex III Par 3 and ESEF 3.1.3 requires an XBRL Report Package but one could not be loaded.
3. [ESEF.RTS.Art.6.a.noInlineXbrlTags] RTS on ESEF requires inline XBRL, no facts were reported.

 

Regards,

Dorota Żukowska

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, ABAP Environment Case 3: Custom CDS View Using ADT
entry.content:
Thanks for the informations given. I have one question regarding API state please:

If I don´t set API state i´m not able to preview data from current CDS view but I will not have any ATC errors while releasing my transport request but if I set my API state, for example, to C1, I will able to test my CDS view but I will have snaphot error ( that I can´t consider as "error" ) from ATC check. 

What is the purpose of Manage API snapshot since most of "errors" showed by ATC are not errors indeed? This is what I´m trying to understand because official help is not so clear to me.

Cheers,
Mauricio Predolim

entry.title: Re: SAC; Story; tryStatement
entry.content: Thank you! this method works for checking if some objects exist or not. In one of the usecases, I check whether a bookmark is applied or not ((Bookmarks.getAppliedBookmark()). Worked like a charm! .

entry.title: SAP Build Apps - API connection expand data entries not works
entry.content:
In the SAP build Apps, We are integrating SAP OData for data extraction. Using 'API_MAINTENANCEORDE' API service and 'MaintenanceOrder' Entity set, we can extract the Order header data. But when we try to extract with expand entries from 'to_MaintenanceOrderOperation', not getting any result.

Working - outputs["MaintenanceOrder"].records[0].to_MaintenanceOrderOperation

Not Working - If use item level, outputs["MaintenanceOrder"].records[0].to_MaintenanceOrderOperation[0]

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite – Access Policies for Integration Packages
entry.content:
Hi @PeterGutsche,

This is a very important feature, an to some customers, critical. Thanks for sharing.

As Chris_75 pointed out, It'd be great if we can control Read accesses to the artifacts. Say, I want the packages belonging to one department invisible to users from another department.

Thanks

entry.title: Document parameters are not correct from sap bo rest api
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to convert sap bo sdk operations to sap bo rest api. So I am using below url to get document parameters;

http://<server>:<port>/biprws/raylight/v1/documents/<DOCID>/parameters

When I try this url to getting document parameters for almost all documents everything is okey and same with sap bo sdk but when I want to list the parameters of some documents, when I run this url, the parameters as in the SDK do not come. Some parameters that say 'Select a context'.

Sample response below;

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="yes"?>
<parameters>
    <parameter optional="false" type="context" dpId="DP0" primary="true">
        <id>0</id>
        <technicalName>0</technicalName>
        <name>Select a context</name>
        <answer constrained="true" type="Text" keyType="Numeric">
            <info cardinality="Single" keepLastValues="true" dynamic="false">
                <lov hierarchical="false" partial="false" refreshable="true" searchable="false">
                    <values>
                        <value id="1">Vehicle Test</value>
                        <value id="2">Vehicle Correct</value>
                    </values>
                </lov>
            </info>
        </answer>
    </parameter>
    <parameter optional="false" type="context" dpId="DP1" primary="true">
        <id>1</id>
        <technicalName>0</technicalName>
        <name>Select a context</name>
        <answer constrained="true" type="Text" keyType="Numeric">
            <info cardinality="Single" keepLastValues="true" dynamic="false">
                <lov hierarchical="false" partial="false" refreshable="true" searchable="false">
                    <values>
                        <value id="1">Vehicle Test</value>
                        <value id="2">Vehicle Correct</value>
                    </values>
                </lov>
            </info>
        </answer>
    </parameter>
    <parameter optional="false" type="context" dpId="DP2" primary="true">
        <id>2</id>
        <technicalName>0</technicalName>
        <name>Select a context</name>
        <answer constrained="true" type="Text" keyType="Numeric">
            <info cardinality="Single" keepLastValues="true" dynamic="false">
                <lov hierarchical="false" partial="false" refreshable="true" searchable="false">
                    <values>
                        <value id="1">Vehicle Test</value>
                        <value id="2">Vehicle Correct</value>
                    </values>
                </lov>
            </info>
        </answer>
    </parameter>
</parameters>

What is the reason for this issue? How can I access the parameters of the same document, which are obtained when triggered with the SDK, via the REST API as well?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors People Analytics (Stories): How to create change report (current vs previous valu
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec your 2024 quest for Kudos inspired me to re-start writting blogs 🙂 Merci pour le commentaire!

entry.title: Integrate ECC EHP 6.3 to IBP for order based planning
entry.content:
I would like to Integrate ECC EHP 6.3 to IBP for order based planning. Since this version of ECC is not capable of using real time integration, the only options are CI-DS and SDI using ERP addon. 

SDI is sunset or going to be sunset, which leaves us with the option of CI-DS. 

IBP team need to generate a distribution plan in IBP using order based planning but CI-DS is used for time series based planning. Can it also be used for order based planning master data and keyfigure data and outbound?

entry.title: Re: How to upload an empty value to remove the existing v...
entry.content: What connector are you using and what type of field are you trying to update? Standard or custom?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hello Afi ! 
Do you know we have a New York group, and we will meet next Saturday for a meetup all together? 

Check this out:
https://community.sap.com/t5/new-york-events/sap-december-2024-meetup-celebrating-the-new-sap-press-book-and-sap-teched/ec-p/13954422#M10

entry.title: Custom development on the HANA stack (essentially HANA XSA) and real world examples
entry.content:
Hi All,

I'm not new to the SAP world but fairly new to this community forum. We have a need in our organization to migrate a custom Java microservice stack to "something" newer. We are considering utilizing the SAP Hana platform (as a technical platform) to refactor and replace the existing code and utilize it ongoing. This is all custom code not related nor interfacing with any SAP objects (database or code). The existing (Java) microservice is very robust, scalable, and serves high volume use cases (it is hit by our website to calculate tax on each order multiple times per order). My question is - has anyone built a similar development in the SAP Hana space and has successfully been working in a production environment for some time? I'm assuming this would be something such as a HANA XSA app but doesn't need to be...but that makes sense to me. We currently are on a S/4Hana (on-prem - AWS) stack as well as a separate SAP CAR instance. We would like to know if this is a viable technical platform to move to from our current Java stack. Any perspectives are very much appreciated! Thanks.

-Matt

entry.title: Re: While trying to select 'Entity Set' in 'Gateway Clien...
entry.content:
I would try using the 'Load Metadata' button for the service in /iwfnd/maint_service of the affected system and then try again.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: CatsXT approval process for time submission
entry.content: Hi, my question is, on CatsXT once i save the time i have inputed, does it auto submit for approval? or do I have take another step to do so ? #catxt

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports for Java SP29 runtime throws an error while genarting report for multiple record
entry.content: Was there any response from SAP about this? This is really super important for us. We are on SP35 and still have this issue. smh.

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content: @c64_fanboy In this case we recommend you to check the Regulatory Change Manager. You can check more information on the session "Legal Changes for HCM Canada" on this blog.

entry.title: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpected token '<', "<!DOCTYPE "... is not valid JSO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to deploy SAP UI5 Fiori application from VS code to ABAP on-premise system. I installed all the extensions needed in VS code to create a Fiori application and was able to preview the application in the local host. When it comes to deployment to the backend system, I get the below error when running the command 'npm run deploy' and deployment fails.  

error abap-deploy-task ZDFGT Deployment has failed.
error abap-deploy-task ZDFGT Change logging level to debug your issue
error abap-deploy-task ZDFGT (see examples https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/tree/main/packages/deploy-tooling#configuration-with-logging-enabled)
error builder:custom deploy-to-abap Unexpected token '<', "<!DOCTYPE "... is not valid JSON
Command deploy failed with error : Unexpected token '<', "<!DOCTYPE "... is not valid JSON

Any suggestions to resolve this error please?

Thank you for your help in advance!

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello,

In note 3515500-YE24: Relevé forms and XML changes for Tax Year 2024, below footnotes are introduced:

  • Box L-11 Rate for the deduction entered in box L-12 or L-13
  • Box L-12 Security option deduction under section 725.2 of the Taxation Act: Benefit granted after June 24, 2024

For L-11, the rate after June 24 is 25% or 33.33% for us, could you please clarify what's the logic behind this box and how to use it?  should a wage type to be generated for that rate(s) on personnel number level? or any specific technical WT(s) should be mapped to it? or any other ways to show the rate(s) for all the eligible EEs?

Also could you please share the logic behind L-12 as well? should the amount of it be calculated via a specific WT at Employee's level? or there's other way to maintain it?

Thanks,

Rory

entry.title: Re: Messages in HOLDING status SAP PO
entry.content: Is it EOIO scenario (Exactly Once In Order)? For this kind of processing one error message can block all others, depending on the queue settings.

entry.title: Request for Free Certification Support
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I have been working as an SAP ABAP developer for 1.5 years and have had the opportunity to work with S/4HANA on a cloud-based implementation project. I am eager to deepen my expertise in this field and believe that obtaining the SAP Certified Associate - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud Certificate would greatly enhance my career prospects.

I recently learned about the Digital Skills Initiative, which offers a free certification attempt. Unfortunately, I missed the application deadline as I discovered this opportunity a bit too late. Despite this, I have already started the learning journey for the certification and am committed to completing it soon.

I would like to request a free certification attempt or a certification voucher to validate my learning and gain better opportunities in the future. Any assistance or guidance on how to proceed would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support!

entry.title: Re: Messages in HOLDING status SAP PO
entry.content:
You can check in below monitoring tool:
Configuration and Monitoring > Monitoring > Adapter Engine > Adapter Engine Status.

Under tab Backlog check general overview of how many messages are in Hold status and for which components, switch to tab Queues and you can check if there are some problems with any queue - search for high numbers in column Number of Entries in Queue. Third tab EOIO Sequences provide detailed information about messages that are in hold status, you can find which message is causing the block there.

I hope it will be enough for you to find source of your issue.

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
@MeghaTufchi Could you kindly check if the form is updated for 2024? It should have a T4(24) on the bottom left corner. If it is not updated to the T4(24) I suggest you to check the KBA 3433906 and also if the version 2 of the note 3539103 is implemented and all the manual steps were performed.
If this is not the case I suggest raising a case so your scenario can be analyzed in detail.

Regarding the T4A entry in V_T799BSFT for Year 2024, I suggest you to check the Regulatory Change Manager. You can check more information on the session "Legal Changes for HCM Canada" on this blog. Also please check constantly the main YE 2024 note mentioned in this blog as well.

entry.title: Re: While trying to select 'Entity Set' in 'Gateway Clien...
entry.content: thanks, but did not work

entry.title: Re: ABAP ADT parallel locking issue for ABAP Classes
entry.content:
This thread was started almost nine years ago, yet the described problem is still not resolved. I cannot believe this long time would not be sufficient for coming up with a fix for this, which I also consider a significant problem of ADT compared to the good old SE80 ABAP workbench that was able to do selective locking for ages.

To get around the problem, I always start editing individual objects in SAP GUI instead of relying on ADT. The interesting thing is that once this happens, ADT is happily accepting it and I can continue changing anything via ADT that is already part of that TR. So, this means it is able to recognize that the changed object is already locked in my transport task, but for some reason it is unable to place the proper lock by itself. This is weird, but then how hard should it be to make this happen?

entry.title: Employee Central Benefit
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I'm trying to Configure the Medical Reimbursement Benefit in Employee Central. After Click on the start claim button I'm facing an error that "In BenefitEmployeeClaim, frequency is not the field or association name". I have also checked all the fields and associations but haven't found a solution.

Error logs found:

"com.successfactors.genericobject.api.exception.ValidationExceptionWithAdditionalDetails: [key=EMPLOYEE_HRIS_GO_INVALID_FIELD_OR_ASSOCIATION, values=[frequency, BenefitEmployeeClaim]] Messages:[key=EMPLOYEE_HRIS_GO_INVALID_FIELD_OR_ASSOCIATION, values=[frequency, BenefitEmployeeClaim]]"

 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Hi , I have added characteristic values in CT04 in DEV , but when transported to qas i dont see
entry.content: What exactly you seen in CT04 in your quality system? Once you entered the characteristics, on the right side, you can see change button. When you click on it, what message do you see?

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Flexible Workflow (WS02000006)
entry.content:
Hi NND

If the system is sending a workitem to a agent (user) from SWIA you can see the pending workitem, the log of the workflow. Or from "flexible workflow administration".

Best regards

 

JCD

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Flexible Workflow (WS02000006)
entry.content:
Hi JCD,

The user is receiving the notification in My Inbox, for the order to either release,reject or rework.

In SWIA the work item is READY to be completed by the approver.

But the system (SAP_WFRT) is automatically releasing the sales order without the user taking any action and still the notification is pending in My Inbox.

entry.content:
Hello everybody,

I recently installed the SAP ABAP Platform trial using Docker and itried to log in but the logon screen after entering DEVELOPER user id and password but keep getting the error in licence check error.

Could someone who has encountered a similar problem advise me if this trial version can still be run?

thank you very much for your help

entry.title: Re: Attachments on Remittance or Payment status information
entry.content:
Hello @Velu_V As far as i know, there is one feature which needs to be enabled on Ariba Network to consume attachments for Payment Remittance. If you are not using CIG or BTP then i guess you need to build the integration using middleware to post Payment Remittance cXML with attachment to AN Buyer ID/URL. 
I think this would require some POC to validate the design & solution

"cXML remittance advice documents can be sent with header-level attachments. Please contact your SAP Ariba Customer Support Specialist to enable remittance attachment"

Remittance Advice Documents | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Vendor Upload File in Ariba Network
entry.content:
Hello @Velu_V 

Main reason for Vendor Upload file (VUF) to setup Enterprise account in SAP Ariba. I guess you can refer below video which explain why and how to use Vendor Upload file.

https://support.ariba.com/item/view/188605

entry.title: Data Migration Cockpit Error : instance already exists in the table
entry.content:
How can the Data Migration Cockpit be configured or utilized to perform a mass upload of material master data for multiple company codes and valuation types in a single file? Currently, attempting to combine records for multiple company codes and valuation types in one upload file leads to staging table errors indicating duplicate key fields, even when no duplicates exist within the file. Is there a way to simplify this process and avoid the need to create separate files for each combination of company code and valuation type?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Cockpit Error : instance already exist...
entry.content: The tool was designed for initial data load specifically, not daily operation. For that, updating entries is not allowed. For objects such as "Product - extend existing record by new org levels", the field "Run ID can" be used to iterate over pre-existing entries but, even so, it has to be done on subsequent runs (please see the attached documentation). In the end, if another entry with the same key fields exist, it will be considered a duplicate and will be skipped.

entry.title: Re: Fiori app Mass change of sales orders returns error '...
entry.content:
Hi, can you check whether OSS 3389957 - Issue with Mass change sales Order not processing more than 2k orders  of any use.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Hide subform based on page count and assign particula...
entry.content:
For benefits of all.

You simply have to put your footer in the last subform of your content.

Do not place it in the master page.

So the last information printed will be the one  containing with this subform.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors "formCompetancy"api access issue? can...
entry.content:
To resolve the issue with accessing or updating data using the SuccessFactors FormCompetency API from CPI, ensure that your query adheres strictly to the specified format. The API expects the following structure:

http://<api server>/odata/v2/FormCompetency(formContentId=17617L,formDataId=7182L,itemId=7L,sectionIndex=4)?$format=json

Parameters in the URL:  The formContentId, formDataId, itemId, and sectionIndex are required fields and must be included in the query string.
These values must be formatted as <fieldName>=<value>L (note the L for each value).

Example Use Case: : https://api5.successfactors.eu/odata/v2/FormCompetency(formContentId=17617L,formDataId=7182L,itemId=7L,sectionIndex=4)?$format=json
To know more please check the link which you have provided :
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/d599f15995d348a1b45ba5603e2aba9b/b6c3612422194ec89bad2457f661b1e0.html#use-case-1%3A-query-the-basic-information-of-a-competency

BR
Surya

entry.title: Dimension Description Text Options
entry.content:
Hi All,

I import Dimension properties from BW for Dimension ID and Description. Example company code and hierarchies:

Dimension ID      Description           H1

1000                   Company1001      North America

North America    North America

In the Stories, I would like to have an alternate description like NA. Is there a solution how to achieved this?

The example above is just one company code, I have hundreds and different hierarchies values. Change it manually is not an option.

Thanks in advance for your idea.

I want to thank you all in advance for helping me in my journey.

I have created port similar to how you have shown in step 5 of this blog. I'm assuming the port created in step 5 is what should be displayed in the receiver port in WE20, correct?

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 SAP GUI and Scripting - Does not work af...
entry.content:
Hi Tamas.

I will like to thank you for your recommendation.
I did update my SAP GUI Windows version to latest version and then still it didn't work so I opened a case for the SAP GUI Team.
And I will like to share that this is a known issue and the bug is caused by Microsoft and SAP is talking to Microsoft about it but until it is solved there is a workaround.

It is described in this SAP note.
3549619 - Windows 11 24H2: SAP Screen Personas scripting in SAP GUI for Windows no longer works

Best regards
Thor

entry.title: Re: How to upload an empty value to remove the existing v...
entry.content:
Hello Mircea_KYN,

Thank you for reaching out. I am not seeing any modifiers for the job description on the Job Posting Template Upload that you can do self-service to force this to be blank. Further ones reserved for other modules such as using '[Blank]' won't work here either.

If you are needing these to be truly blank, your best bet here would be to raise a case with our support team to explore if a BSR can be created and executed to remove the descriptions on the existing JPT's as this is demonstrated to not be available self-service.

Just for a full understanding, what is the need currently to remove all descriptions?

entry.title: 3PR Remittance Evaluation program PC00_M99_URME Reporting details by employees with too many rows
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

We converted to S4 recently and started using the program PC00_M99_URME for Third-party remittance evaluation run.

We have concerns with this program’s reporting. It is populating nearly 20k rows for one pay period wherein the PC00_M99_URMU program used to get less than 100 rows in the report output. We see that URME report is generating the data by employee, output shows the data by employee. But we want the data by the Supplier and Business area only like we had in URMU.

Any help would be appreciated to bring the format like URMU.

URME:

URMU:

We need only one row for Supplier and Bus.A combination. But it is populating it by employee, so we see more rows.

For example, 812 Supplier and 1001 Bus.A should display the total amount in one row and 812 Supplier and 1002 Bus.A in one row like in URMU report below. Same for 810 and 1001, 1002 combination.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Value is required, null given for key: ug!
entry.content: This is very vague. What class or functionality is causing this issue? (I assume this issue is related to wishlist, based on the label.) Do you have a StackTrace? Do you have custom code?

entry.title: Re: Missing mode selector and infinite loading when accessing a Composable Storefront in SmartEdit
entry.content: This is a confusing Q&A. You have both the question and answer in the Question portion. I suggest to move the answer (about smart-edit-feature.module.ts) to a new Answer post. 😉

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content:
do what it asked.

error abap-deploy-task ZDFGT Change logging level to debug your issue

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: The error '<', "<!DOCTYPE "... is not valid JSON has high probability of being an authentication issue. The ABAP system is responding with HTML, possibly for signing in to the request to deploy.

entry.title: is it possible to create sales order with reference of internal order
Re: Attachment in mobile services Can anyone guide me on...
entry.content:
From your metadata you have a three level hierarchy  Equipment > Failure > Files.

So assuming the equipment already exists you could call CreateRelatedEntity to create the failure and associate it to the equipment.  Then call CreateRelatedEntity again to create the files entity relating it to the new failure and finally call UploadStream to store the attachment in the download property of the files entity.  

From the metadata it does not look like you can attach more than one file to the failure so you don't need to worry about looping over the list of attachments.  If it is desired to attach more than one you would need to modify your backend service to support a Collection of files associated to the failure.

entry.title: Re: Process order with DLV status are not settled
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have checked these orders, these are actually settled. We have jobs which are being used to run the settlement process. So these orders were delivered on the evening of 24/11/2024 at 17:15:00 and the job for variances were ran on 23/11/2024 at 22:00:00 and after that the actual settlement job was ran on 24/11/2024 at 01:00:00. So my question is if these orders are delivered on evening of 24/11/2024 at 17:15:00 and the settlement job which was ran earlier in the mid-night of 24/11/2024 at 01:00:00 and completed in 10 mins, how this job settled the orders which are not delivered when the job was running.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Free Trials – Top 5 Picks of Students
entry.content:
As the year ends, I want to express our sincere gratitude to SAP University Alliances for the invaluable support and resources this year. Your commitment to fostering education and innovation has profoundly impacted our students and faculty.

We have greatly benefited from the free trials of SAP solutions, which have provided us with practical insights and experience. The advancements in SAP's AI services have also enriched our knowledge in this ever-evolving field.

Furthermore, the regional events organized by SAP have showcased global collaboration and offered valuable networking opportunities.

Thank you for your unwavering support in education. We look forward to continuing our partnership with SAP University Alliances in the new year. Wishing you happy holidays and a successful new year.

entry.title: Re: is it possible to create sales order with reference o...
entry.content:
Yes, it is possible to create a sales order with a reference to an internal order in SAP ERP1
Define the Reference: Ensure that the internal order is properly defined in the system
Set Up Sales Document Type: Go to transaction code VOV8 and select your sales document type In the "General Control" tab, set the "Reference mandatory" field to the appropriate reference type (e.g., internal order).
Create Sales Order: Use transaction code VA01 to create a new sales order. When prompted, enter the reference to the internal order.

entry.title: Re: reset user password through command line
entry.content:
Hi Sheshman,

In my opinion, R3trans does not support this functionality, I may be wrong.

And even if it is possible, when there are many other reliable and secure ways to achieve this, why go with R3trans? There are DB-specific tools, and SAP applications (frontend) to do what you want to do.

Regards,

Prithviraj

Nishee

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Integration with On-Premise Systems from SAP Basis End #ATR

Hello Ajay,

Really Nice document. Could you please share step by step procedure to connect SAC live and Acquire with S4 Private cloud system. We also raised with SAP but till now no concrete solutions, due to this application build up is completely stuck. It would be great help if you can provide inputs to handle the same.

You are not authorized to query the remote system. Please ask your administrator to grant you the InA role.

 

Regards,

AJ

entry.title: Re: Regarding the sap systems and non-sap systems
entry.content: Hi Luka,

entry.title: SAPience user day - a breath of fresh air
entry.content:
How to simplify your work every day

SAPience represents the Belgian SAP user group. Last Tuesday 03/11/2024 they hosted the SAPience user day in  Salons De Waerboom in Dilbeek. Since one year new leadership is in place and change has been ongoing to transform the user group.

You can find more information on SAPience over on their website: https://sapience.be/

The theme of this year was "How to simplify your work every day".

There are also new, additional board members in place and all of this seems to have led to what I would call a breath of fresh air: new ideas, new initiatives and as a result new outcomes.

I was present together with SAP Partner CNT with whom Aiden partners up for a number of projects since we are often complementary. A collaboration that works very well and creates win-win situations for both parties.

The day kicked off with announcements around SAPience, followed by a short introduction into where SAP stands in terms of strategy by Stijn De Beuckelaer.

Then it was center stage for a lengthier presentation on SAP Business AI by Kai Muehlbauer. You could sense that the AI topic is hot, some of the audience was shifting more towards the tip of their chair, others were wipping out cellphones to take pictures of the slides.

A small demo near the end was in my opinion the most impressive, SAP's Joule AI premium skills used in combination with each other across SAP's product portfolio speaks to the imagination of users on what the future can look like with the help of AI assistance. It screams "this is how it should be" if you would ask me to describe what it looks like. Pretty powerful in other words. The future looks bright.

Break-out sessions by SAP Partners and customers

After the keynote you had multiple tracks of break-out sessions by SAP Partners and customers on how they have simplified their every day work using SAP technology.

Outside of networking, I've attended a number of sessions and what I found was a lot of willingness to share knowledge, a lot of enthusiasm and a large community spirit. Basically what I would expect from a SAP user group where you would find a lot of individuals who really love being active in this space. It was in contrast with my experience of a number of years ago.

Networking opportunities

The enthusiasm from within the sessions also translated into a lot of conversations and networking outside of the sessions. The event was well organized, the venue was accommodating and there was ample room for networking in terms of time divided between sessions and breaks.

Opportunity to grow and call for action

I had the pleasure of meeting a lot of friends there from within the Belgian SAP community. You could state the usual suspects were present but still I was missing some key persons so that's a call for action really.

From what I've seen at the event, there is much more that can happen still in the future given the user group keeps on growing. I've already expressed my interest to get involved in terms of giving knowledge sessions and presentations in the future and being active in the Belgian user group.

You can see the new branding already in the header of this blog and it represents (for me) the breath of fresh air that is now installed in SAPience so a call out to SAP Partners and customers in Belgium: reach out to the SAPience user group if you're not yet a member and consider becoming a member because you can find added value here and strength does also come in numbers in terms of sharing knowledge to each other and discussing amongst peers on best practices and more when it comes to SAP.

entry.title: Re: Is FSCM available in SAP Public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Dave,

Yes, it's available.

Suggest you read the help document first:

Treasury Management | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: Document Type defaulted on FI invoice
entry.content:
Added Finance tag. Please remove other tags.

Suggest you read below Blog for tips of getting your answers faster:

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Process order with DLV status are not settled
entry.content: The settlement job can settle orders that were delivered after the job started but before it finished. This is because the system processes transactions in real-time and updates continuously. So, even if the settlement job started at 01:00:00, it would still include orders delivered up until 17:15:00, as long as the job was still running.

entry.title: Re: 标准功能中的凭证复制功能
entry.content:
Added Finance tag. Please remove other tags.

Suggest you read below Blog for tips of getting your answers faster:

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Hi , I have added characteristic values in CT04 in DE...
entry.content:
The values in CT04 typically follows specific system restrictions since many tasks are handled differently compared to on-premise systems. 

  • Characteristic values entered in CT04 in DEV were not transported to the quality system. 
  • In the quality system, CT04 is non-editable, meaning you cannot add the values directly. This behavior is expected in systems where the configuration is locked to ensure consistency between DEV and QAS.
  • When you navigate to CT04 in the quality system, click the Change button on the right side. Depending on your system setup, you may see one of the following:
    • A message like "Client is not modifiable": Indicates that the quality system is locked for direct configuration changes.
    • Authorization Errors: If you do not have appropriate roles or permissions to modify CT04 entries.
    • No response or greyed-out fields: Signifying that the system restricts this action entirely.

Options to Add PGrp (Purchasing Group) to Characteristic Values:

Option 1: Adjust DEV and Re-Transport

  • Go back to DEV and add the new Purchasing Group (PGrp) to the characteristic values in CT04.
  • Verify that the characteristic changes are included in a transport request and re-transport them to quality.
  • Ensure that the transport includes all relevant dependency objects if applicable.

Option 2: Use Manual Workarounds

  • If transport is not possible or you need an immediate solution, request the system administrator or key user to unlock the quality client temporarily for configuration changes.
  • Once unlocked, you can manually add the PGrp values to CT04 in the quality system.

Option 3: Leverage Master Data Management Tools

  • Use a master data maintenance transaction like CL24N (Assign Object to Class) if applicable.
  • Depending on your setup, tools like MDG (Master Data Governance) can sometimes support data replication or enrichment for characteristics across environments.

Hi @UzeyrOzcan can you tell something abount the OData v4: Offers delta query support?

At the moment I only know that this is supported under the old gateway services. Nothing with RAP or CAP ...

entry.title: Re: reset user password through command line
entry.content: In our system more than 400 users and most of them are keep forgetting or locking their users, half of my day spending to onluck or reset their passwords, i thought that if i can prepare an automation it'll be much more easier to reset their password by sending their username to that automation instead of goto solman and su01 reset & unlock 🙂 Can you suggest some of that other ways so i can work on?

Thanks for the info

Is there a way to maintain isolations needed and perform tagging as in classical wcm functionality
